key_insight: A coupled CEL simulation plus ANN models can accurately predict friction stir welding quality in HDPE and identify process windows that avoid defects while improving thermal and force stability.
engineering_relevance: The workflow gives designers a fast way to tune rotational speed, travel speed, and preheating to produce stronger, more reliable thermoplastic welds.
method: 3D Coupled Eulerian-Lagrangian (CEL) model of HDPE FSW
Two multilayer perceptron ANNs trained on experimental temperature and axial-force data
Process-map generation across rotational speed, welding speed, and bottom preheating conditions
evidence_signal: The ANN models achieved high predictive accuracy with R2 values of 0.91 to 0.99, and the CEL model matched experiments for void formation, thermal response, and mechanical response.
watch_item: The reported optimal window is based on the tested parameter space, so extrapolation to other HDPE grades, tool geometries, or thicknesses may not be reliable without revalidation.
paper_title: Computational and machine learning modelling approaches for weld quality predictions in friction stir welding of high-density polyethylene
